summary: DEC-V61-090 is a well-scoped trust-core micro-PR that correctly narrows M6.1 to the structural boolean flag + override path Kogami's M6 finding 4 specified. Decision-arc coherence is strong (parent_decisions chain to V61-087/089 + spec_v2 + RETRO-V61-001 is explicit), the line-A/B isolation argument is sound, and failure modes are honestly enumerated. Three substantive gaps warrant comment before Accepted: (1) M6.1 cannot mark M6 Done by itself because M6.0 prerequisite is not asserted in the DEC, (2) the case_dir_override field expands the executor contract beyond the M6 finding-4 narrowing without explicit acknowledgement, (3) counter advance assertion is vague where V61-086 Interpretation B canonicalization requires a concrete pre/post number.

findings.title: M6.0 prerequisite not asserted as a hard precondition
findings.problem: ROADMAP M6 sequencing rule (per Notion-Opus advisory finding 1) states 'M6.0 implementation MUST NOT begin until M5.0 has Codex APPROVE recorded' and 'M6 cannot be marked Done while M6.1 is unmerged.' This DEC asserts M6.0 'produces constant/polyMesh/ for imported user cases (commit e207258 · 2026-04-28)' as fact in §Why, but does not explicitly assert M6.0 itself is in an Accepted state with Codex APPROVE recorded — a precondition that this DEC structurally depends on (the polyMesh.exists() guard is meaningless without M6.0's gmsh→polyMesh pipeline). Strategic risk: if M6.0 is later found to have a CHANGES_REQUIRED still pending, M6.1 lands on top of an unstable substrate.
findings.recommendation: Add to frontmatter a `prerequisite_status` field asserting M6.0 commit e207258 has Codex APPROVE on record (cite the codex_tool_report path), or downgrade the §Why claim from 'M6.0 produces …' to 'M6.0 is intended to produce …' with an explicit gate noting M6.1 cannot Accept until M6.0 Accepted is verified.

findings.title: case_dir_override expands the contract beyond M6 finding-4 narrowing
findings.position: §Scope item 1 + 2 (TaskSpec fields and executor case-dir resolution)
findings.problem: The kickoff spec_v2 narrowing per Kogami M6 finding 4 specified 'option-b · structural flag only, no case_kind dispatch.' This DEC introduces TWO coupled fields — `mesh_already_provided: bool` AND `case_dir_override: Optional[str]` — and rewires executor case-dir resolution to use the override instead of allocating a fresh temp dir. The Codex round-1 P2 finding cited in §Scope justifies the expansion well, but the DEC frames this as 'still trust-core micro-PR magnitude' without acknowledging that case_dir_override is a structurally NEW second TaskSpec field with its own validation contract (path must exist + be a directory) and its own failure mode. The narrowing principle in spec_v2 (Kogami M6 finding 4) was explicitly 'flag only.' This DEC expands to 'flag + override path,' which is reasonable but not what the narrowing said.
findings.recommendation: Either (a) explicitly call out that this DEC supersedes the 'flag only' narrowing of spec_v2 with a one-line `supersedes_narrowing` field justifying the expansion + revised LOC envelope, or (b) cross-reference Codex round-1 P2 finding directly in §Scope as the authority for the expansion and confirm Kogami M6 review concurrence isn't required for this widening.

findings.title: Counter advance assertion lacks concrete pre/post number
findings.problem: §Counter impact says 'Estimated counter position after this DEC lands: pulled from git log.' Per W3 blind-control Kogami P2-2 finding (canonicalized in DEC-V61-087 §5 as 'Interpretation B'), STATE.md `last_updated` field is the SSOT and any new DEC's `autonomous_governance_counter_v61` must be based on the latest counter recorded there. STATE.md briefing shows counter v61 53 post-V61-087 W3, with V61-088/089 silent advances per RETRO-V61-001 telemetry. This DEC should explicitly state pre-advance and post-advance counter values, not 'pulled from git log' (which is the Interpretation A path that V61-086 explicitly rejected as canonical).
findings.recommendation: Replace 'pulled from git log' with concrete pre/post numbers derived from STATE.md `last_updated`. E.g., 'Counter pre-advance: 53 (per STATE.md last_updated 2026-04-27T11:10 + V61-088/089 silent advances counted per Interpretation B). Post-advance: <N>.'

findings.title: Notion sync block does not cite V61-088 pre-implementation surface scan
findings.problem: DEC-V61-088 established a routine startup discipline requiring a 2-step pre-implementation surface scan (ROADMAP scan + existing-implementation grep) for any non-trivial implementation work. M6.1 is exactly that scope (≥30 LOC + executor contract change). The DEC does not document that this scan was performed, even though M6.1 maps to ROADMAP M6 (clear roadmap fit) and likely had no prior conflicting implementation. The skip clauses (a)-(d) in V61-088 do not exempt M6.1.
findings.recommendation: Add a §Pre-implementation surface scan section (3-4 lines) documenting (1) ROADMAP scan: maps to M6.1 trust-core micro-PR per spec_v2 narrowing, (2) grep for `mesh_already_provided` / `blockMesh skip` / `case_dir_override` returned no pre-existing implementation. This closes V61-088 governance loop with a one-paragraph artifact cost.

findings.title: kogami_review.triggers list cites RETRO-V61-001 without quoting trigger threshold
findings.problem: First trigger reads 'foam_agent_adapter.py modification >5 LOC (RETRO-V61-001 hard trigger)'. RETRO-V61-001's relevant rule is the Codex-per-risky-PR baseline plus the 'foam_agent_adapter.py >5 LOC' codex trigger (Q3 of original retro). For audit clarity, brief readers may want this referenced more specifically.
findings.recommendation: Append section reference: 'foam_agent_adapter.py modification >5 LOC (RETRO-V61-001 §Q3 codified Codex trigger #4)'. Pure cosmetic.

strategic_assessment: Decision-arc coherence: STRONG. Parent decisions chain (V61-087 governance contract, V61-089 two-track invariant, spec_v2 M6.1 narrowing, RETRO-V61-001 risk triggers) is explicit and the DEC respects each upstream constraint. Roadmap fit: STRONG. M6.1 is a named milestone in ROADMAP, sits on the M6→M7 critical path (M7 cannot be Done while imported cases reach polyMesh consumption without this flag), and is correctly scoped as the 'phase-Done gate' for M6 per Notion-Opus advisory finding 3. Retrospective completeness: ADEQUATE. Failure modes section honestly addresses the four most realistic risks (whitelist accidental flag-set, missing polyMesh, regression, test contract). Out-of-scope hygiene: STRONG. The 4 deferred items (case_kind enum, manifest reading, M7 wiring, snappyHexMesh) are the right exclusions and match the Kogami M6 finding 4 boundary. Strategic risk-vs-benefit framing: SOUND but with the case_dir_override expansion under-acknowledged (P1 finding 2). The 'why structurally safe' section correctly preserves ADR-001 four-plane import direction by arguing the flag is a pure structural boolean and polyMesh.exists() is a filesystem predicate; this is the right framing for the trust-core boundary argument. Overall: ready to merge after the two P1 findings are addressed inline (frontmatter additions, no scope churn) plus the two P2 nits.
